modules.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.MetaMany
documentation: ! /usr/bin/env python

variables.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.MetaMany.__author__
documentation:
This modified version of MetaXcan provides support for analyzing multiple
tissues at with a single run, which is purely for convenience.

Big differences include users specifying one or more tissue databases,
and directing the script to the covariance database directory, working
under the assumption that the covariances will be named nearly identically
to the tissue database, with the exception of extension (and path). This
is true with the current build of GTEx databases found on the box
directory.

Additional parameter changes include:
 * Specifying multiple weight databases
 * Specifying covariance directory, rather than individual files
 * Specifying output directory, rather than named file (results will be
   named identically to the database files, except for extension and
   path.
 * ? (probably others)

Changes in 1.0.0.5:
 * Refactor, cleaned up stale code, and dropped deprecated parameters

Changes in 1.0.0.3
 * Added a version prefix to disambiguate changes in the script to the
   underlying library.
 * To provide more flexibility with tissue database structure, the command
   line interface has changed slightly. For the August 2016 release,
   the default values should work fine. So, you won't need to provide
   --covariance_directory (or if you want to explicitly provide defaults
   for documentation in your scripts, set it to be SAME instead of a
   PATH name)
 * Betas are now written into a subdirectory inside the output directory
   (these are written inside directories named after the tissues) Results
   are written directly inside the output directory and are prefixed as
   follows:
     GWAS-Tissue.csv[.gz]
   Where GWAS is the first gwas filename found inside the gwas directory
   Tissue is the tissue for which the zscores are calculated
   and the optional .gz is there if the --compressed_gwas option is set

modules.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.M03_betas
documentation:
This module contains high level code to parse GWAS/GWAMA summary statistics files and parse them into a standard format.
You can use it as stand alone tool to align it to Predictdb Models or jus tconvert the format,
or it can be called from another script to load the data into memory

TODO:
    Implement "streaming read" where the whole file is not read at once, and output as data is read from file.
    (mostly meant to make the unfiltered operation of this script have less memory footprint)
! /usr/bin/env python

modules.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup
documentation:
Setuptools bootstrapping installer.

Run this script to install or upgrade setuptools.
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._python_cmd
documentation:
Execute a command.

Return True if the command succeeded.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._install
documentation: Install Setuptools.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._build_egg
documentation: Build Setuptools egg.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.archive_context
documentation:
Unzip filename to a temporary directory, set to the cwd.

The unzipped target is cleaned up after.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._do_download
documentation: Download Setuptools.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.use_setuptools
documentation:
Ensure that a setuptools version is installed.

Return None. Raise SystemExit if the requested version
or later cannot be installed.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._conflict_bail
documentation:
Setuptools was imported prior to invocation, so it is
unsafe to unload it. Bail out.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._clean_check
documentation:
Run the command to download target.

If the command fails, clean up before re-raising the error.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.download_file_powershell
documentation:
Download the file at url to target using Powershell.

Powershell will validate trust.
Raise an exception if the command cannot complete.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.has_powershell
documentation: Determine if Powershell is available.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.download_file_insecure
documentation: Use Python to download the file, without connection authentication.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.download_setuptools
documentation:
Download setuptools from a specified location and return its filename.

`version` should be a valid setuptools version number that is available
as an sdist for download under the `download_base` URL (which should end
with a '/'). `to_dir` is the directory where the egg will be downloaded.
`delay` is the number of seconds to pause before an actual download
attempt.

`downloader_factory` should be a function taking no arguments and
returning a function for downloading a URL to a target.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._build_install_args
documentation:
Build the arguments to 'python setup.py install' on the setuptools package.

Returns list of command line arguments.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._parse_args
documentation: Parse the command line for options.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup._download_args
documentation: Return args for download_setuptools function from cmdline args.

functions.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.main
documentation: Install or upgrade setuptools and EasyInstall.

classes.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.ContextualZipFile

methods.name: agentp.software.MetaXcan.software.ez_setup.ContextualZipFile.__new__
documentation: Construct a ZipFile or ContextualZipFile as appropriate.
Supplement ZipFile class to support context manager for Python 2.6.

modules.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc
documentation:
(c) 2014 Brendan Bulik-Sullivan and Hilary Finucane

LDSC is a command line tool for estimating
    1. LD Score
    2. heritability / partitioned heritability
    3. genetic covariance / correlation
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc.sec_to_str
documentation: Convert seconds to days:hours:minutes:seconds

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc._remove_dtype
documentation: Removes dtype: float64 and dtype: int64 from pandas printouts

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc.annot_sort_key
documentation: For use with --cts-bin. Fixes weird pandas crosstab column order.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc.ldscore
documentation:
Wrapper function for estimating l1, l1^2, l2 and l4 (+ optionally standard errors) from
reference panel genotypes.

Annot format is
chr snp bp cm <annotations>

classes.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc.Logger

methods.name: agentp.software.ldsc.ldsc.Logger.log
documentation: Print to log file and stdout with a single command.

Lightweight logging.
TODO: replace with logging module

modules.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.read_header
documentation: Read the first line of a file and returns a list with the column names.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.get_cname_map
documentation:
Figure out which column names to use.

Priority is
(1) ignore everything in ignore
(2) use everything in flags that is not in ignore
(3) use everything in default that is not in ignore or in flags

The keys of flag are cleaned. The entries of ignore are not cleaned. The keys of defualt
are cleaned. But all equality is modulo clean_header().

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.get_compression
documentation: Read filename suffixes and figure out whether it is gzipped,bzip2'ed or not compressed

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.clean_header
documentation:
For cleaning file headers.
    - convert to uppercase
    - replace dashes '-' with underscores '_'
    - replace dots '.' (as in R) with underscores '_'
    - remove newlines ('
')

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.filter_pvals
documentation: Remove out-of-bounds P-values

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.filter_info
documentation: Remove INFO < args.info_min (default 0.9) and complain about out-of-bounds INFO.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.filter_frq
documentation: Filter on MAF. Remove MAF < args.maf_min and out-of-bounds MAF.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.filter_alleles
documentation: Remove alleles that do not describe strand-unambiguous SNPs

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.parse_dat
documentation: Parse and filter a sumstats file chunk-wise

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.process_n
documentation: Determine sample size from --N* flags or N* columns. Filter out low N SNPs.s

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.p_to_z
documentation: Convert P-value and N to standardized beta.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.check_median
documentation: Check that median(x) is within tolerance of expected_median.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.parse_flag_cnames
documentation:
Parse flags that specify how to interpret nonstandard column names.

flag_cnames is a dict that maps (cleaned) arguments to internal column names

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.allele_merge
documentation:
WARNING: dat now contains a bunch of NA's~
Note: dat now has the same SNPs in the same order as --merge alleles.

functions.name: agentp.software.ldsc.munge_sumstats.munge_sumstats
documentation: set p = False for testing in order to prevent printing
